series_description: This series includes but is not limited to: facilities compliance statements, general correspondence, new program applications, certificates of registration, renewal applications, school catalog, new school applications, request for exemption to rules, any other requests or activities of the schools. This file is maintained and updated as long as school is in operation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain original application until school''''s closure

series_description: Financial stability statements (balance sheet) submitted by the schools every two (2) years.
This series includes school correspondence, original school agent applications, cancellation of agents.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain agent files for 2 years after agent is cancelled

series_description: Contract entered into by the department and a person to serve as an Educational Consultant. Shows specific services to be provided, rate of pay, appropriate signatures, etc.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for one year after audit and audit report received

series_description: Also includes other published periodic reports on agency activities.
retention_statement: Paper: Upon publication, fulfill the re- quirements of O.R.C. 149.11

series_description: Formal opinions and contract opinions issued to state agencies with all pertinent materials attached thereto.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until no longer of adminis- trative or legal value to agency.

series_description: Used to certify the destruction or transfer of records according to an approved records retention schedule.
Claims and Litigation Records
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years after case is closed

series_description: Correspondence of the head of an agency (Director, Chief, Secretary, Commissioner, and their deputies), dealing with all aspects of the administration of their offices. Correspondence includes information concerning agency policies, procedures, program,
retention_statement: Paper: Retain four years. Review by State Archives for possible transfer. Destroy if of no historical value to State Archives.

series_description: Includes internal correspondence (letters, memos); also, correspondence from various individuals, companies, and organizations requesting information pertaining to agency and legal intrepreations and other miscellaneous inquiries. This correspondence is
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 2 years, or until audited by the Auditor of State and audit report is released

series_description: Requests for brochures, maps, and other free publications provided to the public by an agency which are answered by stand- ard form letters.
Publications produces by an agency detailing operations, regulations, and/or procedures of an agency.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released. Destroy if superseded and of no historical value to State Archives.

series_description: Includes published reports, unpublished substan- tive reports and policy studies.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until audited by Aduitor of State and audit report is released. Destroy if superseded and of no historical value to State Archives.

series_description: Executive Orders, Proclamations, and Legislative Advisories: Instructions issued by the Governor as the Chief Executive of State Government. (Original copy at Governor's office transferred to State Archives after five years.)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until superseded, obsolete, or replaced

series_description: Used for input in the statewide computer system and to gain approval of all state forms from State Forms Center at DAS Information Management.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain two years after form is obsolete

series_description: Copies of state and federal legislation of interest to an agency.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain two years or until audited by the Auditor of State and audit report is released

series_description: Includes mailing lists, directories, rosters, and registers compiled by an agency.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until superseded, obsolete, or replaced

series_description: Reports created by state agency staff or outside consultants concerning the management or operations of a state agency.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years. Reivew by State Archives for possible transfer. Destroy if of no historical value to State Archives.

series_title: GA-17 Minutes of Agency Staff Meetings
series_description: Minutes and supporting records documenting internal policy decisions.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years. Review by State Archives for possible transfer. Destroy if of no historical value to State Archives.

series_description: Official minutes, orders and directives of a state agency, commission, board, or council.

series_title: GA-19 Newspaper Realeses and News Bullet
series_description: Includes new/press releases and bulletins issued by an agency.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in agency until no longer of administrative value

series_description: Organizational Charts.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released

series_description: Printed matter published by state agencies for fistribution to the public.
retention_statement: Paper: Upon publication, fulfill the re- quirements of O.R.C 149.11

series_description: Records inventory and analysis worksheet: Used for conducting an inventory of an agency's record series. Requests vital information to be used in determining the retention period and disposition of a record.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until superseded by an up- dated inventory or until record series is no longer created

series_description: A form used to identify the length of time a record must be retained before final disposition.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until superseded by a revised retention schedule, or until record series in no longer created

series_description: Used to request installation or modificattion of any equipment or service provided by telephone company.
Listing of long distance calls made by agency personnel for a particular time period.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released

series_description: Manuals produced by an agency for internal per- sonnel training.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until no longer of adminis- trative value

series_description: Accounts receivable ledger.
Worksheet used for proposed budgets.
Application and affidivat for replacement of warrants never received: Form used when warrant does not reach the receiver.
APPLICATION AND AFFIDAVAT FOR REPLACEMENT OF WARRANTS LOST, STOLEN, OR DESTROYED: Used when payee has received a warrant and it has been lost, stolen, or destroyed.
All records which document or are the basis for a payroll including time reports, activity sheets, time cards, overtime, com- pensation time, flextime authorizations, and leave requests.
Form used to change the address or other inform- ation on a warrant.
Bids acknowledgements.
Bids, any type (successful).
Bids, any type (unsuccessful).

Executive Budget Proposal: Table 2-Federal Grants and State Match Detail; Table 4-Continuation, Required or Expanded Proposal; Table 4s-Continuation (C), required (R), Expanded (E); Table 5-Equipment Schedule; Table C-1 Capital Project Proposal.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 1 year after the end of biennium

series_description: Used by agency to maintain a log of batches sub- mitted to CAS.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until batches are verified

series_title: GAF-25 CAS Reports & Inquiries by Data C
series_description: These are various computer generated reports dis- tributed by OBM to various state agencies. They fall into the following class ifications: a. Appropriation; b. Trial Balance; c. Vendor; d. Grant; e. Cap- ital Project; f. Revenue; g. Spending; h. Cash
Cash Receipt Records.

series_title: GAF-29 Claim For Reissuance of Voided Wa
series_description: Forms used to reissue another warrant once a warrant has been voided.
Obligates funds for personal serivce, rental agree ments and capital improvements.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years after expiration of the contract

series_description: Procurement contracts.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years after expiration of the contract

series_description: Used to refer complaints concerning vendor's unsatisfactory products, services, and performance.
Reserves funds and formally recognizes the State's obligation to pay for miscellaneous goods and/or services that require no other formal approval. Used so funds will not lapse.
To request Controlling Board approval of emergency expenditures for Capital Improvement Projects, purchases made without bidwaiver, and transfer of Capital Improvement Funds.
Used by State Accounting to indicate errors on documents returned to an agency site.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until error is corrected

series_title: GAF-38 Delivery Slips and Packing Lists
series_description: Slips and lists showing the name of a vendor, amount delivered, description of the article(s), date of delivery, and method of delivery.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain with notice until audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released.

series_description: Used as a management tool for one-time expenses not covered by DAS Directives.
List of all positions within an agency by position control number, including both filled and vacant positions. Used for budget and personnel purposes.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until superseded by a new roster

series_description: Original computer printout, instructions and work sheets used to complete equipment inventory reports.
Statement of need, signed by Purchasing Officer or Fiscal Officer, justifying the purchase of equipment.

series_title: GAF-44 Federal or State Audit Reports
series_description: Copies of audit reports issued by the Auditor of State or federal government.
Annual financial statements.
Periodic financial statement.
Represents an agency's intent to purchase goods and/or services; it reserves appropriation funds for future spending until the funds are formally obligated (encumbered).
Used to order supplies and/or services and may be used to encumber funds for a purchase from another state agency.
Audit reports and work papers created by an agency's internal audit unit or similar functioning audit.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 2 years after department is audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released

series_description: Authorizes payment to another state agency for goods and/or services received and services as the basis for disbursing funds.
(Accounts Payable Invoices) Bill for goods shipped or servcies rendered. They are matched with purchase orders and rec- eiving reports, and attached to vouchers for payment.

series_title: GAF-55 Lease Records of Lands or Buildin
series_description: Lease records of lands or buildings.
retention_statement: Paper: Fulfill the requirements of O.R.C. 117.49. Retain 5 years after expiration of lease

series_description: Used by OBM to define allocation units and by agencies to define their Spending Authority Codes (SACs).
(Central Office Supply Order Form) May encumber funds for purchases from other state agencies that involve procurement of supplies, equimpent, or standard forms.
Internal form used to obtain authorization for out-of-state travel for a state employee.
Agencies use this form to summarize all out-of- state travel done by personnel for each quarter.
All forms used to authorize deductions for charitable organizations, credit unions, U.S. Savings Bonds, or direct deposit

Computer printout in two parts listing all employees by agency, employee name, social security number and other pertinent payroll information. White copy is corrected and returned to DAS/Payroll Systems. Yellow copy is retained in agency. Payroll lette
Payroll Warrant Cancellation Advise & Transmittal. Used to cancel a payroll warrant issued by an agency in error.
Lists each employee receiving a pay warrant, warrant number, amount paid, and other pertinent payroll information.
Petty Case Records.
Postal Records, Registered Mail, Insured Mail Logs and Metered Records.
Postal Reports, Stamp Requisitions.
Forms used by a person/organization to enter into contract with an agency to request payment for services rendered or to amend contract. May include attendance forms, correspondence, etc.
Used to submit specifications for a printing job to State Printing and to encumber the dollars needed for the job.
Used to record modification of data already est- ablished in the system by a prior printing order.

An order for goods and services by a state agency from a specified outside vendor or seller (other than another state agency). It represents an obligation to pay for goods or services and formally encum- bers the funds to do so.
(Continuation Sheet) Contination sheet for add- itional purchases or contract encumbrance.
(Alteration) Used to modify informaiton already established on a previous purchase order.
Forms used to indicate receipt of goods and/or services.
Used to record receipts or repayment where the original payment was in error, duplicated or made illegally.

series_title: GAF-77 Refund of Expenditure Within the
series_description: Refund of expenditure within the biennium for prior year. Used to record receipts or repayment where the original payment was in error, duplicated, or made illegally. This is done within the biennium for the prior year.

Used to make emergency purchases of all materials, supplies, and equipment and to make direct purchases or engage in contracts of services that might include materials that are not professional, technical, or advisory in nature.
Used for the salvage of equipment or property of the state of Ohio.

Agencies use this form to: A: Request a new re- port. B. Delete a report. C. Change the medium, frequency, or number of copies of reports already received. D. Order an "on request" report (not produced on a regular basis). E. Order a report not re
Request for stop/release of warrant.

series_title: GAF-82 Request for Transfer of General
series_description: Request for Transfer of General Appropriation and Reallotment of Quarters. Allows state agencies to transfer funds between appropriation line items. O.R.c. 127.13
Used to order supplies, forms, foods, etc., from a storeroom.

series_title: GAF-84 Revenue Receipts and Holding Acco
series_description: Revenue Receipts and Holding Account Redistribu- tion. Used to deposit and record revenue receipts from any known revenue source and to hold unknown receipts for redistribution. Also used by agencies which have established a departmental holding fund fr

Used to transfer appropriations at the Spending Authority Code (SAC) level on the Central Accounting System between quarters of the fiscal year if they are within the same appropriation line item of an appropriation bill.
Copies of term contracts entered into by the Division of Purchasing on behalf of all agencies of the state of Ohio.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until superceded by new term contracts

series_description: Used for claiming reimbursement for in-state and out-of-state travel.
Used to establish descriptive information about a vendor or update previously entered vendor data.
Expenditures documents. Authorize payment to a vendor other than a state agency for goods and/or services received.
(Except Payrolls) Used to cancel a warrant issued by an agency in error.
Printouts listing all warrants printed by the Auditor of State.
This series contains requests from students of closed or cancelled schools for help in recovering their tuition.
This series contains records pertaining to schools that have cancelled their certicate(s) of registration.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for four (4) years from date of school closure

series_description: This series contains information submitted by schools pertaining to the Board's Annual Report.
This series contains student records (transcripts, financial aid papers) and student record request forms from when a closed school that are in the possession of the State Board.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain all information for four (4) years after school closes. Thereafter, retain only student transcripts or records of student attendance. Retain transcripts for fifty (50) years. Retain student record request forms for two (2) year.

series_description: This series contains evidence and correspondence gathering during an investigation of a school and includes investigative inquiries, written complaint files, and non-registered schools.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for four (4) years ; Electronic: Retain for four (4) years
